intake.topic_summary: EMIT (not design) the four upstream-proposal markdown files into /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/issues/proposed/. Each file contains: (a) title with raw anchor + workaround pattern label + signal kind + severity request; (b) motivation explaining WHY the workaround exists today; (c) repro pointing at the surviving call site or empirical measurement; (d) proposed signature in hexa syntax that retires the workaround when landed; (e) migration / out-of-scope / evidence anchors sections. The four files map bijectively to raws 144/145/146/147 and to raw 159 NARROW lint matrix rows 5/3/(1+4)/2. Cycle is emission-clean: no .raw/.own/.roadmap mutation, no GitHub gh-issue dispatch (separate cycle), no convergence note edit (the existing r4_raw144to147_upstream_backfill_2026_04_28.convergence note already documents tracking-id placeholders). β qrng_byte=77 → mod 4 = 1 → file declaration order = stdlib-native-write-file (raw 144) FIRST, then aot-cache-source-key-realpath (raw 145), then stdlib-starts-with (raw 146), then stdlib-find-after (raw 147) — i.e. raw-anchor numerical order. mod 5 = 2 → repro section uses concrete file:line pointers (not synthetic snippets) wherever a real failing site exists in hive/nexus/anima. mod 7 = 0 → proposed signatures use pure-hexa syntax (no rust/python pseudocode). γ simbad bridge motif: SIMBAD is an astronomical object reference catalog — every observed object is given a stable identifier so cross-survey citations resolve to the same entity. The four issue files instantiate the same discipline: each upstream-proposal gets a stable filename (= placeholder tracking id slug) so that future cycles citing 'TBD-stdlib-starts-with' resolve unambiguously to one file even before a numeric GitHub id is assigned. α architectural-limit motif: a noise module physical/mathematical/architectural limit IS a falsifier — it bounds what the system can observe. Reframe: each upstream proposal IS an architectural-limit declaration in the language repo (this stdlib gap / this cache invariant breach is a hard limit until lifted). The proposal file makes the limit explicit and citable, exactly as the noise-module limit was made explicit and citable in the absorbed reference cycle.

alpha_absorbed.applied_to_design: Absorbed motif = an architectural limit, once quantified, becomes a falsifier (the system is OK iff it stays inside the limit). Reframe for this cycle: each upstream-proposal markdown IS the explicit quantification of an architectural limit in hexa-lang (raw 144 = ARG_MAX cliff at 1,044,361 bytes on Darwin 25.4.0; raw 145 = file-symlink cache divergence; raw 146 = substring-N != literal-len off-by-one; raw 147 = first-match find on prefix-containing-needle). Each .md file makes the limit citable and the fix mechanically specifiable. Implementation choice: every issue body contains a Repro section with concrete file:line OR empirical measurement pointer — the limit is not abstract, it is the documented breach point.

KICK_BETA_REAL_ANU unset → urandom-fallback honesty label retained.

axis_a_detail.rationale: WHAT — four self-contained markdown documents, no executable artifacts. Each file is filable verbatim into github.com/dancinlab/hexa-lang/issues by a downstream cycle that runs `gh issue create --body-file <path>`. Markdown chosen because (a) it is the upstream repo's expected substrate, (b) raw 8/9 hexa-only-strict prohibits .py/.sh/.rs/.toml/.js but explicitly permits documentation substrates, (c) future-cycle filers don't need to re-render anything — the .md is the deliverable. β qrng (mod 7 = 0) anchors pure-hexa code-fence syntax in the Proposed-signature blocks so the upstream maintainers see the proposed API in their own language.

axis_b_detail.rationale: HOW — every .md follows the same five-section template: (1) H1 title + Summary block citing raw anchor + pattern label + signal kind + severity, (2) Motivation (why workaround exists today), (3) Repro (file:line or measurement pointer), (4) Proposed signature (```hexa fence), (5) Evidence anchors. Uniform shape lets a downstream filer batch-process all four with one renderer. β qrng (mod 9 = 5) anchors the five-section count.
uniform_blocks: Summary (with raw anchor + pattern label + signal kind + severity)
Proposed signature (or 'Proposed fix' for raw 145 single-site change)
axis_b_detail.code_fence_count_per_file: >= 1 ```hexa fence in Proposed-signature; additional fences in Motivation/Repro for current-vs-proposed contrast

axis_c_detail.rationale: WHY — every issue maps to a row in the raw 159 NARROW lint matrix (P1-P5), making the proposal corpus a 1:1 image of the lint harness's pattern matrix. Mapping: stdlib-native-write-file → P5 exec-wrap-native (raw 144); aot-cache-source-key-realpath → P3 realpath (raw 145); stdlib-starts-with → P1 starts_with + P4 substring-mismatch (raw 146 — single helper retires two rows); stdlib-find-after → P2 find_after (raw 147). When all four issues land upstream, the lint harness rows that they retire move from 'fires on legitimate workaround' to 'fires on regression' — closing the upstream-feedback-loop discipline declared in raw 159. γ simbad motif: stable-identifier (filename) ↔ stable-pattern-label bijection lets the lint harness selftest fixtures and the upstream proposals reference each other unambiguously.

issue_to_pattern_row_map.stdlib-starts-with.md: P1 starts_with + P4 substring-mismatch (raw 146 — one helper retires two rows)

axis_c_detail.lint_harness_post_landing_state: all 5 detector rows transition from 'fires on legitimate workaround' to 'fires only on regression' once these issues land upstream and the workaround patches are migrated

fan_out.correlation_estimate: axis_a (markdown emission — substrate choice) ⊥ axis_b (per-file template shape — content discipline independent of substrate) ⊥ axis_c (raw 159 row bijection — semantic mapping independent of either substrate or template). Pairwise: a×b ≤ 0.30 (substrate could swap to .rst/.adoc without changing template; template could shrink to 3 sections without changing substrate); a×c ≤ 0.25 (markdown is irrelevant to lint-row mapping; the bijection holds even if files were emitted as JSON or YAML); b×c ≤ 0.35 (template Proposed-signature section is a vehicle for the bijection but not the bijection itself; the bijection lives in the row-to-file mapping, not the per-file structure). All pairs < 0.7. Verified by: substrate could change (axis a) without breaking template (axis b) or row-mapping (axis c); template could lose Repro section (axis b) without changing substrate or mapping; bijection could expand to P6/P7 future rows (axis c) without changing substrate or template.

axes_surfaced.name: deliverable-substrate-choice

options_considered.option: A — markdown (.md) per file, filable as-is via gh issue create --body-file
B — JSON corpus (single file with 4 issue objects)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: loses 1:1 file-to-issue mapping; downstream filer needs renderer; not the upstream repo's expected substrate.

options_considered.option: C — hexa source (one .hexa per issue with println of body)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: violates ergonomics — markdown is a documentation substrate explicitly permitted alongside hexa; a .hexa file that prints a markdown body is a workaround for a workaround.

options_considered.option: D — direct gh issue create dispatch this cycle (4 actual GitHub issues filed)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: out of declared scope — topic says 'WRITE 4 FILES', not 'FILE 4 ISSUES'; cross-repo write to GitHub requires authentication+approval out of band; corpus emission is the explicit deliverable, dispatch is downstream.

axes_surfaced.decision: Option A — pure markdown, one file per upstream proposal
axes_surfaced.rationale: Matches upstream repo expectation, 1:1 file-to-issue mapping, downstream-filer-batch-friendly, raw 8/9 compliant. The convergence note's @tracking_id_placeholder_format = 'hexa-lang/issues/TBD-<slug>' implies one slug = one filename = one issue.

axes_surfaced.name: filename-slug-source-of-truth

options_considered.option: A — slugs from convergence note @r144/145/146/147_tracking_id (stdlib-native-write-file, aot-cache-source-key-realpath, stdlib-starts-with, stdlib-find-after)
B — slugs from raw anchor numbers (raw144-stdlib-write-file etc.)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: couples filename to raw numbering which can shift; convergence note already declared the canonical slugs as TBD-<slug> placeholders — using a different slug would create a citation drift.

options_considered.option: C — slugs from workaround-pattern-row labels (exec-wrap-native, realpath, starts-with, find-after)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: starts_with row is shared with substring-mismatch (raw 146 covers both via single helper) — single-pattern slug would mis-suggest a 1:1 mapping that the bijection breaks for raw 146.

options_considered.option: D — slugs invented fresh this cycle
options_considered.rejected_rationale: violates raw 47/91 citation discipline — slugs already exist as placeholders; reinventing breaks downstream cycle's ability to resolve TBD-<slug> → file.

axes_surfaced.decision: Option A — convergence-note tracking-id slugs verbatim
axes_surfaced.rationale: Stable identifier discipline (γ simbad motif). Convergence note at /Users/ghost/core/nexus/convergence/r4_raw144to147_upstream_backfill_2026_04_28.convergence already declared @r144_tracking_id=hexa-lang/issues/TBD-stdlib-native-write-file etc. Using the same slugs preserves the citation chain.

axes_surfaced.name: per-file-section-template-rigor

options_considered.option: A — five-section template (Summary, Motivation, Repro, Proposed signature, Evidence anchors) consistent across all four files
B — minimal three-section (Title, Repro, Proposed) — only what the topic literally requires
options_considered.rejected_rationale: loses Motivation (why this matters) and Evidence anchors (raw 91 honesty triad source citations); upstream maintainers reading cold context cannot triage without motivation.

options_considered.option: C — exhaustive ten-section (Title, Summary, Motivation, Background, Current behavior, Expected behavior, Repro, Proposed signature, Migration, Out-of-scope, Evidence)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: section explosion makes each file a long read; upstream issue templates favor terseness — 3-5 levels of header in a single issue is fatiguing.

options_considered.option: D — non-uniform (each file uses whatever sections fit best)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: loses batch-renderer friendliness; downstream filer needs uniform shape to grep title/repro/signature mechanically.

axes_surfaced.rationale: Balances completeness with readability; β qrng (mod 9 = 5) anchors the count; uniform shape lets a downstream filer batch-process; honesty triad evidence array is preserved via Evidence-anchors section.

axes_surfaced.name: tier-1-promotion-gating-for-this-cycle

options_considered.option: A — promote 1 Tier-1 (raw159-upstream-proposal-corpus-4-files-emitted) gated by F1∧F2∧F3∧F4 PASS
B — promote 0 (corpus emission is documentation, not Tier-1-worthy until issues actually filed)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: kick sentinel requires tier1>=1 for PASS; the .md corpus IS the load-bearing artifact upstream filing depends on — promotion-worthy on its own.

inflates promotion counter; the four files are one conjoint corpus deliverable, not four independent Tier-1s — splitting suggests downstream cycles can land them piecemeal which contradicts the bijection axis.

options_considered.option: D — promote 2 (corpus emission + raw 159 lint-row coverage as separate Tier-1s)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: double-counts; the row coverage IS a property of the corpus, not an independent artifact. Conjoint = one promotion.

axes_surfaced.decision: Option A — 1 Tier-1 'raw159-upstream-proposal-corpus-4-files-emitted' gated by F1∧F2∧F3∧F4
axes_surfaced.rationale: Single conjoint deliverable (corpus + uniform shape + bijection) matches one promotion. Honest scope.
